keyPoints: Deadlock detection: Algorithm used to identify whether a deadlock is present in the system.
Banker's algorithm: A deadlock detection algorithm that uses resource allocation matrices to determine if a deadlock will occur.
Worst-case scenario: When all processes are holding resources and waiting for other resources, a deadlock occurs.
Prevention: Avoiding deadlocks by ordering resource allocation and avoiding infinite waits.
Detection: Identifying deadlocks after they occur to prevent system failure.
Recovery: Releasing resources from a process to restore the system to a stable state.
Two-phase abortion: A recovery algorithm that involves aborting processes and releasing resources.
Lock escalation: A technique to recover from deadlock by increasing resource locks.
